riskHeadline: AI governance is investable now, but the moat depends on keeping operator proof fresher than the market.
scoreSignal: Strong demand, strong proof, and strong executive attention are all aligned here.
missingProof: One tighter board memo and cross-sector proof bundle.

riskHeadline: Procurement is one of the safest lanes to scale because evidence reuse is already strong.
scoreSignal: Buyers, trust reviewers, and commercial teams can all consume the same outputs.
missingProof: One tighter benchmark story connecting trust, diligence, and commercial proof reuse.

riskHeadline: Biotech is credible, but should not be overstated as the primary expansion lane yet.
scoreSignal: The vertical signals seriousness and regulated-infrastructure depth to investors and buyers.
missingProof: A tighter benchmark story linking assay, CAPA, specimen, GxP, and safety into one expansion packet.

riskHeadline: FinTech is attractive, but it is easier to oversell than to benchmark cleanly right now.
scoreSignal: Commercial pull is there, but the board story still outruns the proof depth slightly.
missingProof: A tighter cross-lane packet connecting payments, treasury, KYC, and merchant review.

riskHeadline: Nonprofit is strategically useful, but should stay framed as a pattern-strength lane, not the top growth engine.
scoreSignal: The lane proves the operating system can travel across sectors and still stay legible.
missingProof: A better benchmark packet showing how nonprofit patterns map to regulated and commercial lanes.

riskHeadline: Robotics is a useful signal lane, but not a top investment priority yet.
scoreSignal: It helps with range and imagination, but the proof set is not yet dense enough to be a flagship.
missingProof: More repeatable deployment proof and a stronger board-ready value story.
